compartments.notes: Shared soluble phase and metabolic field.

compartments.label: Smooth ER
compartments.notes: Lipid metabolism, Ca storage, CYP detox.

definition.notes: Mixed-authority hepatocyte definition: structural contracts are source-backed where stated; relative 0-1 pools and organelle cycles are schematic and cannot drive quantitative validation.

hepatocyte_radius_um.name: hepatocyte_radius_um
hepatocyte_radius_um.notes: Equivalent-sphere radius derived geometrically from the 3400 um3 reference volume; not a claim that a polarized hepatocyte is spherical.

initial_pool_unit.name: initial_pool_unit
initial_pool_unit.notes: M015 uses normalized pools until quantitative hepatocyte concentrations are curated.

pools.label: ATP
pools.notes: Energy currency available to ATP-costly processes.

pools.label: Intracellular bile acids
pools.notes: Intracellular hepatocyte bile-acid pool; retained legacy id for snapshot compatibility.

pools.label: Canalicular bile acids
pools.notes: Mass-conserving destination for BSEP export; zero is an empty model sink at initialization, not a measured concentration.

pools.label: Intracellular bilirubin conjugates
pools.notes: Intracellular conjugated-bilirubin pool; retained legacy id for snapshot compatibility.

pools.label: Canalicular bilirubin conjugates
pools.notes: Mass-conserving destination for MRP2 export; zero is an empty model sink at initialization, not a measured concentration.

pools.notes: Dominant intracellular cation (~140 mM); sets resting potential.

pools.label: Sodium
pools.notes: Low inside (~12 mM); its gradient powers secondary transport.

pools.label: Magnesium
pools.notes: Free Mg2+ (~0.5 mM); cofactor for ATP-dependent enzymes.

pools.label: GTP
pools.notes: Guanine nucleotide pool for GTPases and biosynthesis.

pools.label: NADP+
pools.notes: Oxidised NADP pool; kept small so NADPH/NADP+ stays high.

pools.label: Oxygen
pools.notes: Cytosolic oxygen availability for mitochondrial and peroxisomal oxidation.

pools.label: Folded secretory cargo
pools.notes: ER quality-control-passed cargo ready for Golgi processing.

pools.label: Autophagy cargo
pools.notes: Damaged protein/organelle material committed to autophagy.

alberts_mbc.notes: Broad cell biology reference for organelle function and cross-organelle stress responses.
alberts_mbc.title: Molecular Biology of the Cell, NCBI Bookshelf

cell_biology_by_numbers.notes: Quantitative reference for sizes, concentrations, copy numbers, and order-of-magnitude checks.
cell_biology_by_numbers.title: Cell Biology by the Numbers

human_cell_proteome_quantitative_2023.notes: Reports a characteristic average hepatocyte volume of 3400 um3 and discusses cell-volume scaling of protein content.
human_cell_proteome_quantitative_2023.title: Quantitative Aspects of the Human Cell Proteome

project_roadmap_07.notes: Project-specific hepatocyte-first engine boundary and stochastic modeling contract.
project_roadmap_07.title: Integrated Cell Engine Roadmap

stochastic_policy.notes: M015 defines the contract; event kinetics are implemented in later process modules.

no calibrated intercellular communication model is attached

brian2_communication.role: optional equation/event backend; never the biological authority

cellular_response.notes: Intracellular-to-canalicular export is mass-conserving. Surface activity is a relative experimental input, not a turnover rate. CYP7A1 feedback and basolateral escape remain absent because the evidence panel does not identify transferable absolute rates. Damage exposure is stress-time (s), not a lesion count. Fate is evidence-only until a matched temporal commitment calibration is supplied.

supported_by: quiescent G0 maintained; no cell-cycle commitment

timing_profile.label: rat hepatocyte post-PHx first-cycle timing
timing_profile.notes: Rat PHx anchor: S phase begins around 18 h and DNA synthesis peaks 21-24 h. S/M durations use generic mammalian anchors until hepatocyte-specific phase durations are added.

basal_glucagon.insulin_context: peripheral insulin infusion designed to reproduce basal portal-vein insulinemia
basal_glucagon.label: Basal portal-equivalent insulin with basal glucagon

limitations: This is a controlled hyperglycemic clamp, not a free-living fed or fasting trajectory.
The benchmark constrains organ-level response direction and magnitude; it does not identify a per-cell rate law.
No acceptance tolerance is invented from the reported group means and SEM values.

lower_glucagon.insulin_context: peripheral insulin infusion designed to reproduce basal portal-vein insulinemia
lower_glucagon.label: Basal portal-equivalent insulin with suppressed glucagon

limitations: Fed peak refers to the measured liver-glycogen peak near 318 min; the 30-min hormone peaks are not assigned to that profile as static values.
The postabsorptive profile may use the measured pre-meal peripheral baseline as context, not as portal receptor exposure.
The prolonged-fast profile remains hormone-data gated.

blockers: The mixed-meal hormones are arterialized peripheral-plasma measurements, not portal-surface exposure.
No matched portal and hepatic-arterial mixing trajectory is loaded.
No adult healthy-PHH INSR or GCGR surface abundance and occupancy-response calibration is loaded.
No matched AKT or cAMP/PKA phosphorylation trajectory is loaded.
Whole-liver glycogen and glucose-output observations do not identify a single-hepatocyte reaction rate.

limitations: Hormones were measured in arterialized peripheral plasma, not portal blood at the hepatocyte surface.
Liver glycogen was measured in study A and full hormonal data in study B under identical protocols, not in the same participants.
Reported cohort means do not define individual trajectories or a continuous interpolation between time points.

events.text: Initialized mixed-authority hepatocyte snapshot; relative pools and organelle cycles are schematic.
Cargo misfolded_secretory_protein lost on er_quality_control->proteasome: success_probability=0.269, edge_stress=0.032, quality=0.240.
Cargo canalicular_bile_transporter retained on golgi->canalicular_face: success_probability=0.601, edge_stress=0.057, quality=0.900.
Cargo lysosomal_hydrolase retained on er_quality_control->golgi: success_probability=0.602, edge_stress=0.045, quality=0.880.

blockers: The requested nine-file Claude Science bundle has not been delivered.
External evidence cannot alter parameters before structural audit and manual primary-source review.

work_packages.unlocks: equation and protocol provenance; never single-cell coupling by itself
reviewable denominator evidence for organ-to-cell conversion
portal exposure and receptor-to-rate coupling candidates

atp_death_switch.notes: The established apoptosis module owns irreversible apoptosis-versus-necrosis commitment.
atp_death_switch.title: Intracellular ATP is a switch in the decision between apoptosis and necrosis

autoencoding_variational_bayes.notes: Original reparameterized variational lower-bound framework underlying VAEs.
autoencoding_variational_bayes.title: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes

bile_acid_mitochondrial_apoptosis.notes: Hydrophobic bile-acid exposure in freshly isolated rat hepatocytes caused ROS, mitochondrial permeability transition, and apoptosis.
bile_acid_mitochondrial_apoptosis.title: Bile acid-induced rat hepatocyte apoptosis is inhibited by antioxidants and MPT blockers

brian2_2_10_1.notes: Audited optional backend version; Brian2 supplies equation/event execution, not biological equations or parameter values.
brian2_2_10_1.title: Brian2 2.10.1

brian2_custom_events.notes: Documents event-triggered interactions suitable for an optional communication backend.
brian2_custom_events.title: Brian2 2.10.1: custom events

bsep_cholestasis.notes: Loss of BSEP function changes hepatocyte bile-acid disposition and sensitizes to cholestatic injury.
bsep_cholestasis.title: Disruption of BSEP Function in HepaRG Cells Alters Bile Acid Disposition

cholestasis_er_stress.notes: Reduced bile-acid transporter expression in hepatocytes produces intrahepatic cholestasis with ER stress.
cholestasis_er_stress.title: Hepatocyte-specific ablation of Foxa2 alters bile acid homeostasis and results in ER stress

evans1976_human_membrane_area_lysis.notes: Human red-cell micropipette measurements reported 2-4% maximum fractional area expansion before lysis (3% mean). This is not a hepatocyte-specific failure measurement; the runtime uses half the lower bound as a conservative engineering cap.
evans1976_human_membrane_area_lysis.title: Elastic area compressibility modulus of red cell membrane

expression_unknown_policy.notes: Gene-specific RNA and protein kinetics remain null until measured or calibrated; generic demo rates cannot silently become hepatocyte parameters.
expression_unknown_policy.title: Genome-expression milestone missing-data policy

fabyan2026_human_liver_3d.notes: Human cleared-tissue 3D reconstruction supports tissue-scale spatial architecture. It does not provide a donor-general hepatocyte mechanics law or justify the canonical polyhedron used by this runtime.
fabyan2026_human_liver_3d.title: 3D reconstruction of human liver tissue at cellular resolution

fujiwara2002_phospholipid_hop_diffusion.notes: Single-molecule tracking in NRK cells measured rapid DOPE motion inside approximately 230-nm compartments and slower macroscopic hop diffusion imposed by the actin-based membrane skeleton. These values are not transferred to human hepatocytes.
fujiwara2002_phospholipid_hop_diffusion.title: Phospholipids undergo hop diffusion in compartmentalized cell membrane

genomic_burst_kinetics.notes: Mammalian alleles transcribe in bursts; enhancer and core-promoter architecture affect burst frequency and size.
genomic_burst_kinetics.title: Genomic encoding of transcriptional burst kinetics

geo_gse289636.notes: Primary repository accession registered by the Peng et al. study for six PHH batches.
geo_gse289636.title: GEO Series GSE289636: primary human hepatocyte single-cell RNA-seq

glucagon_creb_hepatic_control.notes: Supports the hepatic glucagon/cAMP/PKA/CREB-to-gluconeogenic-program direction; no project kinetic constants are inferred.
glucagon_creb_hepatic_control.title: CREB regulates hepatic gluconeogenesis through the coactivator PGC-1

grankvist2024_human_liver_fluxomics.notes: Primary human intact-liver-tissue study combining 13C tracing, spent-medium measurements, mass spectrometry and model-based flux analysis. It supports the required measurement strategy, not numeric transfer into PHH spheroids.
grankvist2024_human_liver_fluxomics.title: Global 13C tracing and metabolic flux analysis of intact human liver tissue ex vivo

gtex_v8.notes: Bulk tissue reference; liver values are not single-hepatocyte molecule counts and require cell-composition-aware interpretation.
gtex_v8.title: NIH Genotype-Tissue Expression project, data release V8

guillou2016_membrane_surface_reservoirs.notes: Micropipette aspiration shows that T lymphocytes can deform at constant volume while apparent surface area increases through membrane-reservoir unfolding. This supports only the kinematic constant-volume principle; no T-cell parameter is transferred to PHH.
guillou2016_membrane_surface_reservoirs.title: T-lymphocyte passive deformation is controlled by unfolding of membrane surface reservoirs

hcv_epigenetic_scar.notes: A defined exposure-specific persistent liver epigenetic scar; not a generic stress-memory rate.
hcv_epigenetic_scar.title: HCV-induced epigenetic changes persist after sustained virologic response

helfrich1973_bilayer_curvature.notes: Separates stretching, tilt, and curvature and supplies the curvature-energy framework for closed fluid bilayers. The project uses the model topology, not an inferred PHH bending modulus.
helfrich1973_bilayer_curvature.title: Elastic properties of lipid bilayers: theory and possible experiments

hepatocyte_connexin32_signal_propagation.notes: Mouse in-vivo evidence that hepatocyte Cx32 gap junctions support intercellular propagation of a glycogen-mobilizing signal.
hepatocyte_connexin32_signal_propagation.title: Defective propagation of signals generated by sympathetic nerve stimulation in connexin32-deficient liver

hepatocyte_regeneration_cycle.notes: Mature hepatocytes are quiescent and can re-enter the cell cycle after priming and mitogenic signaling.
hepatocyte_regeneration_cycle.title: Cellular and Molecular Basis of Liver Regeneration

hmdb_2022.notes: Curated human blood/plasma concentration reference ranges; specimen compartment is retained explicitly.
hmdb_2022.title: HMDB 5.0: the Human Metabolome Database for 2022

honka2018_human_liver_glucose_uptake.notes: 18F-FDG PET plus euglycemic-hyperinsulinemic clamp in 326 participants without diabetes; whole-liver tissue denominator.
honka2018_human_liver_glucose_uptake.title: Insulin-stimulated glucose uptake in skeletal muscle, adipose tissue and liver: a positron emission tomography study

human_bile_acid_death_mode.notes: High GCDC exposure produced predominantly oncotic necrosis in primary human hepatocytes and HepaRG, unlike apoptosis-dominant rat responses.
human_bile_acid_death_mode.title: Mechanisms of bile acid toxicity in human and rat hepatocytes

human_bsep_taurocholate_2013.notes: Taurocholate transport by recombinant human BSEP in inverted Sf9 membrane vesicles.
human_bsep_taurocholate_2013.title: Early identification of clinically relevant drug interactions with the human bile salt export pump

human_cyp7a1_feedback.notes: Primary human hepatocyte data support bile-acid-dependent CYP7A1 repression and show that human regulation is not reducible to a single FXR-SHP edge.
human_cyp7a1_feedback.title: Bile acids and cytokines inhibit the human cholesterol 7-alpha-hydroxylase gene via the JNK/c-Jun pathway

human_fxr_shp_regulation.notes: Supports human FXR-to-SHP regulation and the SHP/LRH-1 links to CYP7A1 and NTCP regulation.
human_fxr_shp_regulation.title: Human FXR regulates SHP expression through direct binding to an LRH-1 binding site

human_glycogen_hormone_clamp_1996.notes: Healthy-young-men hyperglycemic clamp with somatostatin, matched insulin exposure and manipulated glucagon; liver glycogen synthesis and turnover were measured by in-vivo 13C NMR.
human_glycogen_hormone_clamp_1996.title: The roles of insulin and glucagon in the regulation of hepatic glycogen synthesis and turnover in humans

human_hepatic_transit_1996.notes: Tracer transit-time model fitted in eight normal volunteers; reports portal flow fraction and liver mean transit time.
human_hepatic_transit_1996.title: An anatomic and physiological model of hepatic vascular system

human_hepatocyte_proteome_2016.notes: Quantitative LC-MS/MS of purified hepatocytes from seven human liver donors using Total Protein Approach and Proteomic Ruler.
human_hepatocyte_proteome_2016.title: In-depth quantitative analysis and comparison of the human hepatocyte and hepatoma cell line HepG2 proteomes

human_hepatocyte_renewal.notes: Retrospective carbon-14 birth dating; population renewal is not a fixed single-cell lifespan.
human_hepatocyte_renewal.title: Diploid hepatocytes drive physiological liver renewal in adult humans

human_hepatocyte_somatic_mutations.notes: Somatic sequence variants are stable physical records; the model does not infer an individual variant without data.
human_hepatocyte_somatic_mutations.title: Single-cell analysis of somatic mutations in human hepatocytes

human_hnf4a_cyp7a1.notes: Supports HNF4A as a major positive CYP7A1 transcriptional regulator and parallel FXR/SHP and FGF19 feedback mechanisms.
human_hnf4a_cyp7a1.title: Retinoic acid represses CYP7A1 expression in human hepatocytes and HepG2 cells

human_liver_adenylates_1992.notes: HPLC measurements in control human liver tissue: ATP, ADP, AMP, total adenine nucleotides, energy charge and NAD+.
human_liver_adenylates_1992.title: Can adenine nucleotides predict primary nonfunction of the human liver homograft?

human_liver_atp_synthesis_2008.notes: In-vivo 31P magnetization-transfer measurements in nine healthy volunteers; the reported rate is apparent Pi-to-ATP exchange.
human_liver_atp_synthesis_2008.title: Quantitative ATP synthesis in human liver measured by localized 31P spectroscopy using the magnetization transfer experiment

human_liver_cell_atlas.notes: Single-cell RNA-seq of about 10,000 cells from nine human donors supports donor-aware hepatocyte heterogeneity and zonation.
human_liver_cell_atlas.title: A human liver cell atlas reveals heterogeneity and epithelial progenitors

human_liver_cell_atlas_2019.notes: Human single-cell RNA-seq supports portal-central hepatocyte heterogeneity and canonical zonation markers.
human_liver_cell_atlas_2019.title: A human liver cell atlas reveals heterogeneity and epithelial progenitors

human_liver_glutathione_1980.notes: Total glutathione measured in six histologically normal human liver biopsies, reported per hepatic protein.
human_liver_glutathione_1980.title: Glutathione in human liver in normal and pathological conditions

human_liver_glycogen_mixed_meal_2000.notes: Serial in-vivo human hepatic glycogen measurements around a mixed meal.
human_liver_glycogen_mixed_meal_2000.title: Direct assessment of hepatic glycogen metabolism in humans with 13C NMR spectroscopy

human_liver_glycogen_mrs_2012.notes: In-vivo human liver tissue measurement; glycogen was quantifiable in 25 participants.
human_liver_glycogen_mrs_2012.title: Liver metabolite concentrations measured with 1H MR spectroscopy

human_liver_glycogen_overnight_1996.notes: Overnight-fasted healthy volunteers measured with localized 13C MRS.
human_liver_glycogen_overnight_1996.title: Natural abundance 13C NMR measurement of human liver glycogen

human_liver_glycogen_starvation_1973.notes: Human liver biopsy glycogen after prolonged starvation or carbohydrate-poor diet.
human_liver_glycogen_starvation_1973.title: Liver glycogen in man: the effect of total starvation or a carbohydrate-poor diet followed by carbohydrate refeeding

human_liver_mps_oxygen_zonation_2017.notes: Human liver MPS under controlled approximately 3-13% oxygen; supports directional zone functions, not an in-situ human sinusoidal pO2 value.
human_liver_mps_oxygen_zonation_2017.title: Control of oxygen tension recapitulates zone-specific functions in human liver microphysiology systems

human_liver_multiome.notes: Human liver single-nucleus RNA plus ATAC data support joint expression/chromatin state while preserving donor and cell-type context.
human_liver_multiome.title: Liver single-nucleus multiome profiling reveals cell-type mechanisms for cardiometabolic traits

human_liver_phosphorus_mrs_2017.notes: Healthy-volunteer liver spectroscopy; reports the 1.054 kg/L liver density used for wet-tissue concentration conversion.
human_liver_phosphorus_mrs_2017.title: Phosphodiester content measured in human liver by in vivo 31P MR spectroscopy at 7 tesla

human_liver_single_cell_proteomics_2025.notes: Healthy human single-hepatocyte proteomics across 20 portal-central bins; 413 cells from 14 individuals.
human_liver_single_cell_proteomics_2025.title: Single cell spatial proteomics maps human liver zonation patterns and their vulnerability to fibrosis

human_liver_spatial_atlas_2026.notes: Human spatial transcriptomics, MERFISH and protein validation; reports species-specific portal-central expression programs.
human_liver_spatial_atlas_2026.title: A spatial atlas of the healthy human liver from live donors

human_mixed_meal_endocrine_1996.notes: Healthy-human mixed-meal study. Study B measured arterialized peripheral plasma glucose, insulin and glucagon and used a tracer approach for hepatic glucose output; study A measured liver glycogen under the same nutritional protocol in a separate cohort.
human_mixed_meal_endocrine_1996.title: Direct assessment of liver glycogen storage by 13C NMR and regulation of glucose homeostasis after a mixed meal

human_mixed_meal_homeostasis_1996.notes: Healthy human in-vivo serial liver glycogen, hepatic glucose output and pathway-contribution measurements after a mixed meal.
human_mixed_meal_homeostasis_1996.title: Direct assessment of liver glycogen storage by 13C NMR and regulation of glucose homeostasis after a mixed meal

human_mrp2_abundance_2012.notes: Targeted LC-MS/MS of liver membrane fractions from 51 human donors.
human_mrp2_abundance_2012.title: Interindividual variability in hepatic expression of the multidrug resistance-associated protein 2 (MRP2/ABCC2)

human_ntcp_uptake_2003.notes: Taurocholate uptake kinetics in fresh and cryopreserved human hepatocytes; cryopreservation caused large activity variability.
human_ntcp_uptake_2003.title: Function of uptake transporters for taurocholate and estradiol 17beta-D-glucuronide in cryopreserved human hepatocytes

human_tki_bile_acid_trajectory.notes: Human SCHH data distinguish intracellular from extracellular bile-acid responses and show drug-specific CYP7A1 feedback; not a generic BSEP-knockout calibration.
human_tki_bile_acid_trajectory.title: Novel Bile Acid-Dependent Mechanisms of Hepatotoxicity Associated with Tyrosine Kinase Inhibitors

kemas2021_phh_glucose.notes: Two-donor PHH 3D-spheroid study; Table 1 and the methods were reviewed directly. Validation-only outside the same culture format.
kemas2021_phh_glucose.title: Insulin-dependent glucose consumption dynamics in 3D primary human liver cultures measured by a sensitive and specific glucose sensor with nanoliter input volume

koenig2012_author_executable_reencoding.notes: Pinned author-maintained SBML Level 3 re-encoding with 36 kinetic laws and permissive redistribution terms embedded in the file; its source repository is MIT licensed. It is audited as a secondary executable artifact, not silently substituted for the official PLOS supplement.
koenig2012_author_executable_reencoding.title: Author-maintained executable SBML re-encoding of the hepatic glucose model

koenig2012_hepatic_glucose_model.notes: Primary publication for the 49-metabolite, 36-reaction liver model and its reported glucose-dependent phosphorylation and pathway-switch benchmarks.
koenig2012_hepatic_glucose_model.title: Quantifying the contribution of the liver to glucose homeostasis: a detailed kinetic model of human hepatic glucose metabolism

koenig2012_plos_dataset_s2.notes: Exact CC BY publication supplement. Structured inspection finds 36 reactions and zero kineticLaw elements, so this artifact is not executed.
koenig2012_plos_dataset_s2.title: Koenig et al. 2012 Dataset S2: SBML model

koenig_glucose_glycogen_figure_script.notes: The trace described as glycogen 250 mM selects the first nonuniform-grid value greater than or equal to 250 mM, which is 276.6667 mM.
koenig_glucose_glycogen_figure_script.title: Author MATLAB glucose-glycogen figure analysis

koenig_glucose_glycogen_protocol_script.notes: The repository script fixes external lactate at 0.8 mM, integrates for 200 minutes, and uses a nonuniform glycogen grid that does not contain 250 mM.
koenig_glucose_glycogen_protocol_script.title: Author MATLAB glucose-glycogen dependency protocol

koenig_glucose_model_author_repository.notes: Pinned author repository used only for model-lineage and protocol auditing. No explicit repository license was found, so its legacy executable is not vendored.
koenig_glucose_model_author_repository.title: Author repository for the Koenig hepatic glucose model

libroadrunner_2_9_2.notes: Pinned optional runtime used only to regenerate the shadow-validation artifact.
libroadrunner_2_9_2.title: libRoadRunner 2.9.2 SBML simulation engine

lodish1983_hepg2_secretory_transit.notes: Pulse-chase evidence for selective ER-to-Golgi transit in human HepG2 hepatoma cells. It establishes pathway structure and system-dependent rates; it is not a numeric PHH calibration.
lodish1983_hepg2_secretory_transit.title: Hepatoma secretory proteins migrate from rough endoplasmic reticulum to Golgi at characteristic rates

marion2013_human_sch_bile_acids.notes: Table 4 reports endogenous bile acids and experiment-level BEI aggregates from four human sandwich-cultured hepatocyte donors.
marion2013_human_sch_bile_acids.title: Endogenous Bile Acid Disposition in Rat and Human Sandwich-Cultured Hepatocytes

ncbi_gene_records.notes: Coordinates use annotation release RS_2025_08 on GCF_000001405.40.
ncbi_gene_records.title: NCBI Gene, Homo sapiens GRCh38.p14 annotation

ncbi_grch38_p14.notes: GRC/NCBI chromosome lengths and RefSeq accessions. Chromosome lengths are placed-scaffold lengths including estimated gaps.
ncbi_grch38_p14.title: Human Genome Assembly GRCh38.p14

ncbi_mtdna_rcrs.notes: Revised Cambridge Reference Sequence; 16,569 bp reference molecule.
ncbi_mtdna_rcrs.title: Homo sapiens mitochondrion, complete genome (NC_012920.1)

nist_glucose_molar_mass.notes: NIST formula C6H12O6 and molecular weight 180.1559 g/mol; used only for the explicit mg-to-umol conversion.
nist_glucose_molar_mass.title: NIST Chemistry WebBook SRD 69: Glucose

olander2021_human_hepatocyte_size.notes: Direct isolated-human-hepatocyte measurements: median diameter 18.4 um across 54 cryopreserved batches; 88% of cells were 12-26 um. The value does not establish in-situ liver-plate geometry.
olander2021_human_hepatocyte_size.title: Hepatocyte size fractionation allows dissection of human liver zonation

peng2022_phh_requirements_standard.notes: Published CSCB standard source for ALB and HNF4A >=90 percent PHH product-marker criteria.
peng2022_phh_requirements_standard.title: Requirments for primary human hepatocyte

peng2025_phh_quality_attributes.notes: Table S2 and Figure S2B report batch-resolved FACS marker fractions and scRNA-seq cell-type composition for six commercial PHH batches.
peng2025_phh_quality_attributes.title: The validation of quality attributes in Primary Human Hepatocytes Standard

phh_bile_acid_gene_regulation.notes: Primary human hepatocytes support FXR-SHP and FXR-FGF19 activation, CYP7A1 repression and BSEP induction; dose/time observations must not be converted into unsourced kinetic constants.
phh_bile_acid_gene_regulation.title: Potency of individual bile acids to regulate bile acid synthesis and transport genes in primary human hepatocyte cultures

primary_hepatocyte_isolation_metabolism_.notes: Tracks energetic and redox metabolites from in-situ liver through hepatocyte isolation and culture; isolation substantially changes ATP, NADP(H), GSH and GSSG.
primary_hepatocyte_isolation_metabolism_.title: From in vivo to in vitro: Major metabolic alterations take place in hepatocytes during and following isolation

primary_hepatocyte_protein_turnover.notes: Primary human hepatocyte proteins have widely different half-lives; no universal protein-decay constant is defensible.
primary_hepatocyte_protein_turnover.title: Systematic analysis of protein turnover in primary cells

rawicz2000_bilayer_elasticity.notes: Micropipette measurements separate low-tension undulation smoothing from direct high-tension bilayer area stretch and report a mean direct area-stretch modulus of 243 mN/m across the tested PC bilayers. The runtime does not treat that model-bilayer modulus as a PHH cortex law.
rawicz2000_bilayer_elasticity.title: Effect of chain length and unsaturation on elasticity of lipid bilayers

reactome_adherens_junctions.notes: Curated human calcium-dependent cadherin trans-dimerization and junctional linkage to the cytoskeleton.
reactome_adherens_junctions.title: Reactome: Adherens junctions interactions

reactome_hgf_met.notes: Curated human HGF/MET activation and downstream PI3K/AKT, RAS and STAT3 branches.
reactome_hgf_met.title: Reactome: Signaling by MET

reactome_il6_signaling.notes: Curated human IL6/IL6R/gp130/JAK/STAT1-STAT3 and RAS-MAPK pathway.
reactome_il6_signaling.title: Reactome: Interleukin-6 signaling

reactome_insulin_receptor.notes: Curated human pathway: INSR autophosphorylation, IRS/SHC recruitment and PI3K/RAS signaling.
reactome_insulin_receptor.title: Reactome: Signaling by Insulin receptor

scgen_perturbation_model.notes: VAE-based perturbation-response modeling with explicit out-of-sample evaluation.
scgen_perturbation_model.title: scGen predicts single-cell perturbation responses

scvi_single_cell_generative_model.notes: scVI models single-cell expression probabilistically while accounting for count noise and batch effects.
scvi_single_cell_generative_model.title: Deep generative modeling for single-cell transcriptomics

scvi_tools_documentation.notes: Current probabilistic single-cell modeling software boundary; not installed as a core dependency.
scvi_tools_documentation.title: scvi-tools documentation

singer_nicolson1972_fluid_mosaic.notes: Establishes the thermodynamically fluid lipid matrix with amphipathic integral proteins. It is an architecture reference, not a hepatocyte diffusion coefficient or mechanics calibration.
singer_nicolson1972_fluid_mosaic.title: The fluid mosaic model of the structure of cell membranes

stuschke_bojar1985_rat_hepatocyte_membra.notes: FRAP in intact isolated rat hepatocytes at 21 C reported probe- and condition-dependent lipid diffusion and an unselected-protein mean. It is retained only as hepatocyte-context evidence that both classes move laterally; it is not a healthy-human parameter.
stuschke_bojar1985_rat_hepatocyte_membra.title: Insulin effect on translational diffusion of lipids and proteins in the plasma membrane of isolated rat hepatocytes

uniprot_p02768.notes: Reviewed human ALB entry; canonical precursor is 609 residues and is processed to a mature form.
uniprot_p02768.title: UniProtKB P02768: human albumin

upr_proteostasis.notes: ER stress activates UPR; unresolved stress can transition from adaptation to pro-apoptotic response.
upr_proteostasis.title: The involvement of ER stress in bile acid-induced hepatocellular injury

usp_human_albumin_reference_standard.notes: Compendial reference reports the 585-residue mature albumin composition and 66,438 Da mass.
usp_human_albumin_reference_standard.title: USP rAlbumin Human reference standard

wilson2003_human_hepatocellularity.notes: Human hepatocellularity and microsomal-protein scaling anchors corrected for processing loss; not a single-cell geometry study.
wilson2003_human_hepatocellularity.title: Inter-individual variability in levels of human microsomal protein and hepatocellularity per gram of liver

limitations: The five commercial PHH batches are not a healthy in-vivo population reference interval.
Only derived BEI percentages are published in Table S7; paired A_Ca and A_CaFree values and uncertainty are unavailable.
BEI combines uptake, intracellular retention, canalicular export, canalicular geometry and calcium-sensitive junction behavior.
The assay does not isolate a BSEP turnover rate or active surface copy number.
The CSCB 30 percent criterion is a PHH product-quality criterion, not a simulation pass threshold.

limitations: The six batches are commercial cryopreserved PHHs, not a healthy in-vivo population reference interval.
Each endpoint is a mean plus SD for n=3 within-batch replicates; Table S4/S5 does not label the replicate type, and donor population variance is not estimated by those SD values.
Source-reported 0.0 values are interpreted as undetectable/censored, not as proof of zero enzyme activity.
Raw timepoints, LLOQs, covariance, viable-cell trajectories, donor genotypes and absolute CYP protein abundances are unavailable.
SCR and MFR are assay-level aggregate outputs and do not identify transport, binding, CYP turnover, POR coupling or metabolite-loss kinetics.

The CSCB criterion is stated for representative drug metabolism with CYP3A4/testosterone as the explicit example; it is not generalized here to every CYP and is not a physiological range or simulation pass threshold.

reported_associations.finding: Both SCR and MFR were reported as highly correlated with matched CYP1A2 and CYP3A4 mRNA levels.
reported_associations.model_consequence: Association does not identify an expression-to-activity rate law.

limitations: The measurement operator transforms a model output into the published observation space; it is not a biological rate law and supplies no parameter values.
The Kemas aggregate endpoint is a signed net medium balance and cannot identify bidirectional transport or intracellular pathway fluxes by itself.
The Grankvist isotope-tracing study demonstrates a measurement strategy in intact human liver tissue, not a transferable numeric calibration for PHH spheroids.
No synthetic trajectory, fitted parameter, acceptance threshold or model-state coupling is exported.

quantity_audit.reason: The assay directly constrains signed net glucose amount lost from the medium per seeded cell over each reported time window.
Net medium disappearance does not separate inward from outward glucose transport.
Donor-level net production proves that outward glucose flux can contribute, but the pooled medium endpoint does not quantify it separately.
Extracellular net glucose change does not measure intracellular glucose phosphorylation.
Medium glucose alone cannot resolve glucose-6-phosphate dephosphorylation from transport or other glucose-producing routes.
Net glucose disappearance does not report what fraction enters glycogen.
Net production can arise from glycogenolysis or gluconeogenesis and the current endpoint does not distinguish them.
Medium disappearance does not distinguish glycolysis from glycogen synthesis, pentose-phosphate flux or intracellular accumulation.
A signed net endpoint cannot allocate glucose production between gluconeogenesis and glycogenolysis.
No isotopologue or intracellular product measurement separates pentose-phosphate use from other glucose fates.
The supplement establishes a donor-dependent sign difference qualitatively, while Table 1 pools the healthy insulin-sensitive records and does not expose a numeric donor trajectory.
Low-insulin media also contain 100 nM glucagon, whereas glucagon in high-insulin media is not reported as a measured zero.
The reported denominator is seeded cells; window-specific viable-cell counts are unavailable.

limitations: Marker-positive FACS fractions and transcriptomic cell-type fractions are different measurement constructs and are not interchangeable purity estimates.
The six commercial PHH batches do not define a healthy in-vivo hepatocyte population distribution.
Non-hepatocyte fractions describe the composition of a PHH product; they are not organelles or state fractions inside one simulated hepatocyte.
Published cell-type percentages are rounded and may sum to 99.99 or 100.01 percent.
Five hepatocyte subsets were reported, but a machine-readable donor-by-subset numeric matrix is not curated here.
Six batches are insufficient for donor-disjoint generative-model training or causal donor-effect estimation.
The 600 pg value is a seven-donor cohort average; the paper does not publish an uncertainty for this headline value.
The 3000 um3 cell volume is model-derived from an assumed average cellular protein concentration of 200 g/L, not a direct morphometric measurement.
Compartment percentages are protein-mass fractions, not organelle volume fractions, membrane areas, or renderer geometry targets.
The reported 8.7 billion molecules is an aggregate protein-molecule estimate and does not define a complete molecule-by-molecule inventory.
The combined 9400-protein study dataset must not be interpreted as exactly 9400 proteins present in every hepatocyte.
Static average abundance does not identify synthesis, degradation, trafficking, crowding, or donor-specific dynamics.

surfaces.limitations: Four-donor day-7 sandwich culture is not in-vivo physiology. Paired-buffer values use an estimated intracellular volume, source proxy zeros are censored observations, and aggregate BEI cannot be reconstructed from group means.

surfaces.action: require donor-resolved PHH synthesis plus compartment-resolved ER, Golgi, medium and degradation time courses before activating a constitutive secretion model
surfaces.limitations: The PHH ELISA endpoint identifies cumulative extracellular albumin only. Published HepG2 pulse-chase evidence supports pathway topology but cannot supply PHH translation, ER export, Golgi maturation, exocytosis or degradation rates.

surfaces.limitations: The official supplement is non-kinetic; the permissively licensed current executable re-encoding reproduces two of five precision-derived publication benchmarks and represents mean liver behavior rather than a donor or zone-resolved hepatocyte.

surfaces.action: retain the four-way protocol matrix; never substitute the unlicensed legacy executable for the vendored runtime
surfaces.limitations: The later author-repository lineage reproduces five reported targets under recovered conditions, but the repository protocol conflicts with the paper legend and exact publication-artifact equivalence remains unresolved.

surfaces.action: retain the unit-normalized HGO residual and 16 blocked PHH targets without assigning pass/fail; require matched dynamic context for validation
surfaces.limitations: The liver-scale model and human HGO estimate have unmatched time and boundaries. The PHH spheroid observations have no same-format model prediction, and the delivered model-only curve is not held-out human validation.

surfaces.action: retain source-backed pathway topology and the measured PHH insulin response; require receptor abundance and quantitative kinetics before activation
surfaces.limitations: A defined 1.7 uM insulin challenge has matched pAKT and PCK1/G6PC responses in PHH spheroids, but INSR surface abundance, receptor occupancy and complete kinetics remain unavailable.

surfaces.action: use engine-owned convex surfaces for signed gap, broad contact-patch geometry, membrane-domain labels, enter/stay/exit inputs and volume-preserving contact deformation; enforce the explicit 1% cross-system area-strain cap; do not use elapsed contact time as a causal parameter; keep force, adhesion, junction gating and biochemical activation blocked
surfaces.limitations: The 18.4 um diameter is measured for isolated PHH batches and sets only an equivalent-volume scale. The regular truncated-octahedron boundary and affine deformation are kinematic proxies, not donor tissue morphometry or PHH rheology. One percent is an engineering cap equal to half the lower 2% human-RBC lysis bound. Constant-volume T-lymphocyte deformation supports only the kinematic principle and supplies no PHH parameter. Computed patch area and deformation cannot identify adhesion bond density, force, stiffness, receptor occupancy or gap-junction permeability.

surfaces.action: require the pinned backend plus source-backed equations, complete units, calibrated parameters and geometry coupling
surfaces.limitations: Brian2 is an equation/event executor, not a source of hepatocyte biology. No calibrated communication model is attached.
